description_long: This is a vintage copper diving helmet, characterized by its large, rounded dome and wide, flared base. The helmet's surface is heavily patinated with greenish oxidation and brownish rust, giving it an aged, weathered appearance. The front features a prominent circular viewport with a metal grid, flanked by two smaller side ports, all rimmed with thick metal. The top of the helmet has a rectangular grated vent, and there are several protruding knobs and rings around the base, likely for attaching hoses or securing the helmet. Notably, the back of the helmet displays three distinct red claw-like scratch marks, adding a dramatic visual element. The overall construction suggests it is made primarily of metal, designed to protect the wearer in underwater environments.
description: A weathered, vintage copper diving helmet with a domed top, circular front viewport, and greenish patina. The helmet features red claw marks on the back and multiple metal fittings.
description_view_0: Side view showing the large circular side port, patinated copper surface, and part of the flared base.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the helmet's rounded back, flared base, and three red claw-like scratch marks.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with another circular port and visible attachment rings on the base.
description_view_3: Front view displaying the main circular viewport with a metal grid, two side ports, and the grated vent on top.
attribute_ventilation_pattern: One rectangular grated vent on the top front of the helmet; no other visible vents.
attribute_graphic_design: Aged patina with three red claw-like scratch marks on the back; otherwise plain with no additional graphics.
